assumptions: Assume the question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.
Assume the dividend-related receivable is a recognised foreign-currency-denominated intragroup monetary item.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including qualifying intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
